title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. stellatum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. stellatum, also known as the sulfur buckwheat or desert buckwheat, is a perennial plant species in the buckwheat family native to the western parts of North America. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. stellatum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage, making it a valuable herb for cardiovascular health and antioxidant support. The plant can be prepared for herbal use by making a tea from its leaves and stems, or by infusing it in oil for topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
